# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve, serveFile } from "https://deno.land/std@0.145.0/http/mod.ts";
import { permissions } from "https://deno.land/std@0.145.0/permissions/mod.ts";
import { fetch as denofetch } from "https://deno.land/x/dotenv/mod.ts";
import { request } from "https://deno.land/x/request@v6.0.0/mod.ts";

const port = 8000;

// Fetches data from the provided URL using Deno's request API
async function fetchUrl(url: string) {
  const response = await request(url);
  if (response.ok) return response.body!.readToString();
  throw new Error(`Request to ${url} failed with status ${response.statusText}`);
}

const handleRequest = async (req: Request) => {
  if (req.method !== "GET") {
    return new Response("Method not allowed", { status: 405 });
  }

  const urlParam = new URLSearchParams(req.url.search);
  const url = urlParam.get("url");
  if (!url) {
    return new Response("URL parameter is required", { status: 400 });
  }

  try {
    await permissions.request({ name: "net" });
    const content = await fetchUrl(url);
    return new TextResponse(content);
  } catch (e) {
    console.error("Failed to grant network permission:", e);
    return new Response("Failed to establish network connection", { status: 503 });
  }
};

serve(handleRequest, { port });
